title: lithocarpus plumbeus
intro: Lithocarpus plumbeus, also known as the blue beech, is a species of evergreen tree native to the mountains of Taiwan. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The blue beech is rich in compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Lithocarpus plumbeus is typically prepared as a decoction or tea by infusing its leaves or bark in hot water, and can also be used in the form of a tincture or powder for medicinal purposes.
